cases.name: Assignment tests
cases.description: Automated tests in test_assignment.py — they check gpa.py and numbers.py.

cases.name: Internal tests
cases.description: Automated tests in test_internal.py — they check add.py and hello.py.

cases.name: Code style & readability
cases.description: Code is for computers AND humans. Humans should be able to read and understand the code you write.

levels.label: Exceptional
levels.description: Idiomatic, readable, well factored. Has well placed comments and docstrings.

levels.label: Acceptable
levels.description: Code works but hard to follow. Might be over commented or under commented. Might be missing docstrings.

levels.label: Unacceptable
levels.description: Code is Unreadable / unstructured. Human cannot understand it.

cases.name: Reflection
cases.description: Now that you've completed the assignment, think about what you did and share your thoughts. What did you learn? What confuses you? Where did you struggle? Where might you need more practice? A good reflection is **as specific as possible**, **uses the terminology of the problem domain** (what was learned in class / through readings), and **is actionable** (you can pursue next steps, or be aided in the pursuit).

levels.label: Excellent
levels.description: Well written, clear, and actionable. Uses terminology of the problem domain. Specific about what was learned and where more practice is needed.

levels.label: Needs Improvement
levels.description: Missing one of: well written, actionable, clear, uses terminilogy specific to assignment, demonstrated what was learned.
